title: Two massive triangles facing each other across a precise gap. The gap is not empty space — it is the contract. Precept's definition is the exact width of that governing aperture: only what fits through is structurally valid. The triangles are immovable walls; the passage between them is determined by the geometry of the definition, not by a runtime guard. Prevention is not a check — it is the shape.
conceptualBridge: The opposing triangles mean Precept's definition is the governing aperture because only configurations that fit through the exact gap are structurally valid — the triangles make every other possibility physically absent.
decision: Shortlisted: strongest radical departure this round — new visual family, new jade-green palette, exceptional visual economy and monochrome scores, distinct silhouette unlike any other candidate in the set.
